description:
A capacity-planning analyst has left a single raw telemetry file under  
  /home/user/logs/resource_usage_2023-08-04.log that contains point-in-time
  CPU, memory and network utilisation of a host during 4 August 2023.

  Your assignment is to create a **daily roll-up CSV** that the analyst’s
  dashboard can ingest.

  1. Read the file **/home/user/logs/resource_usage_2023-08-04.log**.  
     Each line is already in the following format (exactly one space between
     every token):

        YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS CPU=<number>% MEM=<number>MB NET_IN=<number>kB/s NET_OUT=<number>kB/s

     Example line (the first real record in the file):
        2023-08-04 00:00:00 CPU=17% MEM=2048MB NET_IN=120kB/s NET_OUT=80kB/s

  2. Using any mixture of awk, sed (and other coreutils if helpful), strip the
     unit-suffixes ( “%”, “MB”, “kB/s” ) so that only bare integers
     remain; keep the original ordering of the fields.

  3. For each of the four metrics
        • CPU(%)  
        • Memory(MB)  
        • Network_In(kB/s)  
        • Network_Out(kB/s)  
     compute:
        a) the minimum value of the whole day  
        b) the maximum value of the whole day  
        c) the arithmetic mean rounded to **one decimal place** (e.g. 45.4).

  4. Write the results to **/home/user/output/usage_summary_2023-08-04.csv**.
     The directory /home/user/output/ may not exist, so create it if needed.

  5. The CSV **must match this exact layout** (including the header line, the
     comma separators, capitalisation and ordering).  
     No extra spaces before or after commas, and every line ends with a single
     trailing newline:

        Metric,Min,Max,Average
        CPU(%),<min>,<max>,<avg>
        Memory(MB),<min>,<max>,<avg>
        Network_In(kB/s),<min>,<max>,<avg>
        Network_Out(kB/s),<min>,<max>,<avg>

     Example of the desired final file (with the correct numbers substituted):

        Metric,Min,Max,Average
        CPU(%),4,89,45.4
        Memory(MB),1024,8192,4300.8
        Network_In(kB/s),12,1024,384.8
        Network_Out(kB/s),8,840,295.6

  6. When you are finished, list the contents of /home/user/output/ so
     the grader can see the new file, and optionally `cat` the CSV to the
     console to show its contents.

  Hints:
     • sed is handy for deleting fixed suffixes;  
     • awk can accumulate sums and track extrema;  
     • you do NOT need any non-standard packages or root privileges.  
     • The grader will only verify the presence and the exact byte-for-byte
       contents of /home/user/output/usage_summary_2023-08-04.csv.
truth:
Initial files provided to the agent
  -----------------------------------
  1) /home/user/logs/resource_usage_2023-08-04.log

     Exact contents (five lines, each terminated with a single LF):

       2023-08-04 00:00:00 CPU=17% MEM=2048MB NET_IN=120kB/s NET_OUT=80kB/s
       2023-08-04 06:00:00 CPU=65% MEM=4096MB NET_IN=512kB/s NET_OUT=340kB/s
       2023-08-04 12:00:00 CPU=89% MEM=8192MB NET_IN=1024kB/s NET_OUT=840kB/s
       2023-08-04 18:00:00 CPU=4% MEM=1024MB NET_IN=12kB/s NET_OUT=8kB/s
       2023-08-04 23:59:59 CPU=52% MEM=6144MB NET_IN=256kB/s NET_OUT=210kB/s


  Target artefact that must exist after the agent finishes
  --------------------------------------------------------
  1) /home/user/output/usage_summary_2023-08-04.csv

     Exact required byte content (each line terminated with a single LF):

       Metric,Min,Max,Average
       CPU(%),4,89,45.4
       Memory(MB),1024,8192,4300.8
       Network_In(kB/s),12,1024,384.8
       Network_Out(kB/s),8,840,295.6

  The automated checker will:
     • verify that the file path exists,
     • read the file and compare it byte-for-byte with the block above,
     • fail the task if there are any extra spaces, different rounding,
       missing/extra newlines, or lines in the wrong order.
name: task_000000_e38d3219
